CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
July 24, 1990
Eugene M. Feerick
Super intendent
Barstot Unified School Distri.ct
551 South Avenue rrHrl
Barstow, cA 923Lt
Re3 Your District's conflict of Interest code
our File No. I-9 0-3 66
Dear lIr. Feerick:
This is in response to your May 10, 1990, letter requesting
advice coneerning the conflict of interest provisions of the
Po1itical Reform Act (the trAct").1/ Your reguest is one for
general assistance. Accordingly, we are treating it as a request
for informal assistance. z /
Your board has been confronted with a concern from a
citizen's activist group which maintains that your board members
are required to report their spouse's incone on Form 730,
Statenent of Economic Interests. 
This group has also stated that
your newly approved conflict of interest code does not conform to
Commission regul-ations.
You have asked the Fair Political- Practices Commission (the
rrcommissiontt) to review the conflict of interest code for the
Barstow Unified school District (the "Districtr'). 
Additiona]Iy,
you have requested advice regarding the reporting duties of the
board membeis as designated employees within the District's 
code.
We will address each question separately.
l/ 
covernment code sections 81000-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated.
Courmj.ssion regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations
Section 18OOO, et seq. Al-1 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Reg'ulations.
2/ 
Infornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imnunity provided by an opinion or fornal written advice.
(Government code section 83114; 2 cal. Code of Regs. Section
1832e (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8o7 o sacramento cA 958o4-o8o7 . (916)322-5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-336
Page 2
OUESTION #1
You trave asked the Commission to review the District's
conflict of interest code and to offer any suggestions for
inprovement.
we have had an opportunity to review the District's 
code and
have the fotlowing to offer to assist you in updating your code.
1. Since the school district 
incorporated Commission
Regutation 2 cal. code of Regs. section 18730 as the body of its
code, iten 3, the filing of the disclosure statements, can be
deleted in its entirety. 
This infornation is set out in
Reg:ulation 18730 and is unnecessary here. Some of the infornation
you have set out is not accurate and confLicts with the sarne
provisions contained in the regulation.
2. 
Your code does not provide for disclosure of financial
interests by consultants for the agency. The definition of
designated enployee includes consultants. (Section 82019.) A
consultant is any natural person who provides under contract,
information, advice, recornmendation or counsel to any agency.
(Regulation 18700. )
The Cornmission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the naking of decisions on behalf of public agencies. Rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-uaking capacity, you may use a speciaLized disclosure
category which provides that the disctosure reguired of
consultants sha1l be deteruined on a case-by-case basis by the
superintendent for the district. 
As the superintendent, you nay
rnake a determination as to lrhat disclosure, if any, is required by
any particular consultant.
The position rr consul-tant* rr is added as the last designated
position. 
The asterisk would refer the reader to this footnote:
*consul-tants sha11 be included in the list of designated
employees and shall dj.sclose pursuant to the broadest
disclosure category in the code subject to the following
l irnitation :
The superintendent rnay deternine in writing that a
partj.cular consuLtant, although a itdesignated position,tr is
hired to perform a range of duties that is linited in scope
and thus is not required to fu1ly conply rrith the disclosure
requirernents in this section. such written determination
sha1l j.nclude a description of the consultant's duties and,
based upon that description, a statement of the extent of
discl.osure requirenents. The superintendent,s deternination
is a public record and shall, be retained for public
inspection in the sane manner and location as this conflict
of interest code.


3. 
Conflict of interest codes typically include Iega1
counsel to public agencies in the list of designated positions.
At a Dinimurn, Iegal counsel should be considered a I'consultantrr to
the district. 
However, if the district presently retains 1ega1
counsel, we would prefer that the code specifically include the
titLe lega1 counsel in the list of designated positions.
4. 
Government code section 87302 requires disclosure
categories to specifically require the disclosure of business
positions. 
Each of your disclosure categories should be amended
to read as follows:
category 1(a): Interests in reat property which is located
in whole or in part either (1) within the boundaries of the
district, 
or (2) within two miles of the boundaries of the
district, 
including any leasehold, beneficial or ownership
interest or option to acquire such interest in real property.
category 1(b): 
Investments and business positioDs in,.or
souries- of income from persons or business entities which are
contractors or sub-contractors which are or have been engaged
in the performance of building construction or design within
the district.
category 1(c): 
rnvestlnents and business positions in, or -
sources of income from persons or business entities engaged
in the acquisition or disposal of real property within the
j ur j.sdict ion .
category 2: Investments and business positions in, or
sources of income frorn business entities which nanufacture,
se11 or provide supp15.es, materials, books, rnachinery,
servj.ces or equipment of the type utilized by the department
for which the designated employee is manager or director.
Our Fi■e No. ■-90-336
Page 3
Category 3:
Invest'ments and business positions in, or
sources of i-ncorne from business entities which are
contractors or subcontractors engaged in the perfornance of
work or services of the type utilized by the dePartment for
which the designated employee is rnanager or director.
5. within each disclosure category you shoufd remove
any language referring to monetary threshofds or definitions of
terms. Renoving this language will prevent your agency from
having to make unnecessary and tine consuming code amendrnents in
the future. 
ff you were to keep this language in your code, you
lrould be required to anend your code each and every time that a
monetary threshofd lirnit is statutoriJ.y arDended. AL1 monetary
thresholds and definitions are contained in Regulation 18730 uhich
is already part of your code.
5. The code requires persons assigned categories 2 and 3 to
disclose interests under category 1. Your code currently is


our File No. I-9 0-3 3 5
Page 4
requi.ring that the positions of Director, Personnel Coromission,
Manager, Transportation, the Purchasing Agency and the Supervisor,
Food services, report full disclosure as is required of the Board.
If that is your intention, then merely assign categories 1, 2 and
3 to those positions and delete the last sentence in categories 2
and 3. You may need to revie!, this issue further rrith the Board
to see what its intention was when assigning disclosure
categories.
7. Upon review of the list of designated positions, we
guestion why the position of Director, I,laintenance, operations,
Transportation and safety has been given a linited disclosure
requirement. It seems, based on the job titIe, 
that this Position
should be assigned categories 2 and 3 as rrel1 as category 1.
OUESTION #2
You have aLso asked if your board nembers are required to
report their spouse's incorne on the Form 730, Statement of
Economic Interests.
CONCLUSION
The income of a spouse is required to be reported on the
statement of economic interest if the spouse's emptoyer is a
reportable source of income as provided in the disclosure category
portion of the code. If the employer is a reportable source of
incorne, the reporting requirenents would be 50* of the conmunity
property interest in the sPouse's income. Refer to Regulation
1ez3o(l) (5) (B) and footnote 5 rrhich is incorporated as part of
your code for further infornation regarding disclosure of sources
of income.
ANALYSTS
Gov. code Section 82030 defines trincomet' to include, "... 
any
comraunity property interes'E in the i.ncome of a spouse. tr Cornrnunity
property interest is defined as 5ot interest in your spouse's
incorne it ttre empJ-oyer is a reportable source of income as defined
in your conflict of interest code.
This reporting requirement is not new. It lras created in the
original Political 
Reforrn Act of 1974. There is a new provision
within section a73o2, which now requires that sources of income
within the last 12 months be reported in all assurninq office
statenents. However, annual statenents have always been required
to include reportable sources of income. Again, this requirenent
would not affect your spouse's income unless the ernployer is a
reportable source of incone.


`         Our Fi■e No. エー90-336
Page 5
SH:CAH:■90366
Enclosures
Please do not hesitate to contact ne at (91-5) 322-59OL if you
should have questions regarding the above advice.
S incerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel

By: Ch-ery1 A. Hoff

property interest in the sPouse's income. Refer to Regulation

your code for further infornation regarding disclosure of sources
of income.
